title: erythroxylum campestre
intro: Erythroxylum campestre, also known as the 'campestre coca' or 'wild coca', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the Erythroxylum genus, which is closely related to the coca plant (Erythroxylum coca) but contains less cocaine and more alkaloids with medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as pain relief, fever reduction, and improved digestion. Erythroxylum campestre can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although its use is often limited due to its potential side effects.
